title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit led the Atlanta Braves to a 3-2 win over the Boston Red Sox.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo homers but pitched five innings with six strikeouts.
Boston's Marcelo Mayer tied the game with a solo home run in the seventh before falling in extra innings.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Reunion between Patriots and Stefon Diggs is unlikely due to looming trade for A.J. Brown, according to Albert Breer.
Diggs, interested in returning, posted a 1,000-yard season for Patriots; financial constraints impede his re-signing.
A.J. Brown trade would add a $32 million contract, complicating Patriots' cap space for Diggs' potential $10 million deal.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen near Blue Hill Avenue, Dorchester, around 1 p.m. Tuesday, according to Boston police.
Boston police describe LaGrant-Gomes as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, brown eyes, and black hair, known to visit several city areas.
Detectives are actively investigating the case and urge anyone with information to contact District B-3 or submit an anonymous tip.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The shooting occurred on May 10 at 69 Kingsdale St., leaving the woman hospitalized with life-threatening injuries.
Broomes charged with assault with intent to murder; held without bail pending a May 21 dangerousness hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox starting pitchers have a combined 2.74 ERA and a 1.08 WHIP in 39 starts in 2026.
Mookie Betts and Xander Bogaerts remain productive, with Betts batting .171 and Bogaerts .257 for their respective teams in 2026.
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 18 home runs in 2026, after being available in free agency without Boston re-signing him.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony, 22, has been out with a right wrist sprain since May 4.
Anthony, who was placed on the 10-day injured list May 7, no longer wears a wrist brace after receiving a cortisone injection.
Anthony has resumed throwing but hasn't started swinging a bat; he feels positive about his recovery progress.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally drove into BrightPath Norwood Child Care Center; no injuries were reported.
The crash led to the daycare's temporary closure for structural safety assessment.
Driver's error occurred while dropping off a passenger, accidentally hitting the accelerator.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish the 2026 regular season with a 10-7 record.
The team's challenging schedule includes games against Seattle, Denver, and Chicago after a favorable 2025 schedule.
Despite potential roster improvements, analysts suggest Patriots may win fewer games than last year's 14-3 record.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short,' a documentary on Martin Short, is now available on Netflix.
'Mars Attacks!' is streaming for free on YouTube and Plex, providing a comedic take on alien invasions.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2, hosted by Colin Jost, is now streaming on Netflix with duo teams.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Wu's $4.9 billion budget proposal faces potential rejection from Boston City Council members seeking increased spending.
Wu stands firm on budget limits, citing a $50 million deficit and fiscal responsibility as reasons against inflating revenue.
City Council must decide on budget amendments by June 10 or Wu's proposal will automatically take effect.
